metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the Remediation AC and ADR-contents bullet for fan-out: either (a) replace the `batch.extra_criteria` language with 'the dynamic Pass-1 fan-out appends project-criterion descriptors via `ProductionBatchRunner.route_criteria` without modifying `gates/plan-review.yaml` or the v3 schema' — or (b) document a v4 schema path and mark it out-of-scope, removing the AC.
findings.finding: Remediation AC bullet says 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list', but the body's 'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism' section explicitly states that a new `batch` field is IMPOSSIBLE because the v3 schema has `additionalProperties:false` and is immutable, and instead adopts the ProductionBatchRunner approach with no schema change. The ADR-contents bullet also references 'the additive `batch.extra_criteria` fan-out', compounding the contradiction. An implementer cannot simultaneously pass both the stated AC and follow the body's design decision.
findings.location: Remediation AC bullet 2 ('The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list…') vs. body section 'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism (clears G6/T1/T3)' and ADR-contents bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the contradicted remediation AC with: '- [ ] The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out appends activated project-criterion descriptors via `ProductionBatchRunner` (calling `route_criteria(ctx)`) without adding any field to the v3 `batch` schema or modifying `gates/plan-review.yaml`; a test asserts a project criterion runs alongside built-ins in Pass-1.' Update the ADR-contents bullet to match.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the contradiction: update the ADR contents description and AC remediation item #2 to state that fan-out uses `ProductionBatchRunner`-level injection (NOT `batch.extra_criteria`, which is schema-prohibited), so the ADR documents the actual mechanism.
findings.finding: The ADR contents description says it will document 'the additive `batch.extra_criteria` fan-out', and AC remediation item #2 requires 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list' — but the fan-out mechanism section explicitly states `batch.extra_criteria` is IMPOSSIBLE (schema is immutable, additionalProperties:false) and the actual mechanism is ProductionBatchRunner-level injection. The ADR as described would document the wrong pattern, and AC #2 contradicts the chosen design.
findings.location: ADR contents section ('the additive `batch.extra_criteria` fan-out') and AC remediation item #2 ('uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list') vs. fan-out mechanism section ('batch.extra_criteria ... is impossible')
findings.suggested_fix: In the ADR contents bullet, replace 'the additive `batch.extra_criteria` fan-out' with 'the ProductionBatchRunner-level project-criterion injection (why `batch.extra_criteria` was ruled out, and how built-in vs. project descriptors are deduped)'. Update AC remediation #2 to match: 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out appends project criterion descriptors via ProductionBatchRunner after assemble_context, without modifying the static gates/plan-review.yaml or the v3 batch schema.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify, co-located with the `_resolve_criteria` contract, exactly how project-prefixed descriptors reach `BatchRunRequest.criteria`: does `ProductionBatchRunner.run()` call `effective_criteria(repo_root)` independently and append descriptors before calling `run_pass1`, or does `route_criteria` return them and they flow through a different path? Define the lookup protocol that avoids the `by_id().get(cid) == None` silent-skip for project ids.
findings.finding: The processing protocol for how `project.<name>`-prefixed descriptors reach `_resolve_criteria` (and thus `BatchRunRequest.criteria`) is never co-located with the code that needs it. The plan states the runner 'appends ONLY the `project.<name>`-prefixed descriptors … to the finder's tier-split set', but `_resolve_criteria` currently calls `registry.by_id()` which only returns `CANONICAL_LLM` descriptors. The plan does not specify: (a) whether project descriptors are pre-loaded into `by_id()` via `effective_criteria`, (b) whether they arrive in `req.criteria` from the YAML, or (c) whether `ProductionBatchRunner.run()` fetches them independently from `effective_routing`. No lookup path is co-located with `_resolve_criteria`'s contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define, in the ADR or the plan's overlay schema section, the exact field vocabulary for a `project.<name>` routing entry inside `plan_review: {…}`: which fields are required (`exec`?), which are optional with their defaults, and which packaged-schema fields are forbidden or not applicable.
findings.finding: The overlay schema's value vocabulary for the `plan_review` gate-key inner object is never fully defined. The plan states the overlay is 'gate-keyed (`{plan_review: {...}, activate: [...]}`)' and 'reusing the packaged routing schema', but the packaged `criteria_routing.json` schema has no gate-key wrapper — it is a flat `{cid: {exec, facet, applies_at, …}}` dict. What fields are valid inside `plan_review: {…}` for a project criterion entry (must it have `exec`? `applies_at`? `facet`? are these optional or required?), and what does 'reusing the packaged routing schema' mean for the inner object, is never co-located with the load-time validation rule that would enforce it.
findings.location: § .rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay per schema (What section) and § ADR contents

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay format is a **new gate-keyed schema** (`{plan_review: {<id>: routing_entry, ...}, activate: [...]}`) distinct from the flat packaged `criteria_routing.json` format, and specify the overlay JSON Schema document separately in the ADR and CI gate (do not claim 'reusing the packaged routing schema' if the top-level structure differs).
findings.finding: The plan claims it is 'reusing the packaged routing schema' for the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay, but the overlay's described top-level structure (`{plan_review: {...}, activate: [...]}`) is gate-keyed and structurally different from the actual packaged `criteria_routing.json` (a flat `{criterion_id: routing_entry}` object with no gate key and no `activate` list). The 'schema reuse' claim is misleading: implementers following it will not find a schema they can directly reuse, and the CI drift/parity gate validator described in the plan would need a new schema document for the overlay format, not the packaged one.
findings.location: What section: '.rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay' bullet; also Scope clarification / ADR contents sections
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section and ADR contents, replace 'reusing the packaged routing schema' with 'each criterion entry under the `plan_review` gate key uses the same per-criterion routing object shape as the packaged index (exec/applies_at/block_threshold/etc.), but the overlay file as a whole has a new top-level gate-keyed envelope `{plan_review: {...}, activate: [...]}` that requires its own JSON Schema document for the CI validation gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC9 with a criterion that verifies the `ProductionBatchRunner` fan-out path: e.g. 'An activated project criterion is appended by `ProductionBatchRunner.route_criteria` to the finder's tier-split set, deduped against built-ins, without modifying `gates/plan-review.yaml` or the v3 batch schema.'
findings.finding: AC9 (remediation block, second bullet) states the fan-out mechanism is 'an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list', but the plan body (§'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism') explicitly states that a new `batch` field like `extra_criteria` is IMPOSSIBLE because the v3 schema has `additionalProperties:false` and is version-pinned — and instead mandates the `ProductionBatchRunner` approach. The AC therefore describes a mechanism the plan simultaneously rules out, so it either verifies the wrong thing or is a dead letter.
findings.location: Remediation AC block, bullet 2: 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list…'; contradicted by §'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism (clears G6/T1/T3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC9 bullet with: 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out for project criteria is implemented in `ProductionBatchRunner` (not via a new `batch` schema field): `route_criteria(ctx)` returns the full effective set, and only `project.*`-prefixed descriptors are appended to the finder's tier-split set, deduped against built-ins; `gates/plan-review.yaml` and the v3 schema are unchanged; a test confirms a `project.*` criterion runs end-to-end without modifying the gate YAML.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the contradiction in remediation AC #2: replace 'uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list' with the correct mechanism — 'uses `ProductionBatchRunner.route_criteria(ctx)` to append project-prefixed descriptors without touching the static `criteria:` list or the immutable v3 batch schema.'
findings.finding: Remediation AC #2 directly contradicts the body: AC says 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list', but the body explicitly states that a new `batch` field is impossible under the immutable v3 schema and that fan-out goes through ProductionBatchRunner instead — an implementer following the AC will build the wrong mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions) — second bullet; 'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism' section of the body
findings.suggested_fix: Replace remediation AC #2 with: 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out appends project-criteria descriptors via `ProductionBatchRunner.route_criteria(ctx)`, leaving the static `criteria:` list in `gates/plan-review.yaml` and the immutable v3 batch schema untouched; a test asserts that an activated project criterion produces a finding without any schema change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct Remediation AC #2 to replace the `batch.extra_criteria` claim with the actual chosen mechanism — `ProductionBatchRunner`-side injection of project criterion descriptors after `assemble_context` — since `batch.extra_criteria` is impossible under the immutable v3 schema (`additionalProperties:false` on `$defs/batch`).
findings.finding: Remediation AC #2 asserts a `batch.extra_criteria` fan-out mechanism that the plan body explicitly rules out as infeasible without a v4 schema bump. The v3 schema's `$defs/batch` is `additionalProperties: false` (confirmed in `workflow.v3.schema.json` line 288), making `extra_criteria` an impossible field addition — yet the AC still requires it, contradicting the body's stated resolution of using `ProductionBatchRunner`-side injection instead.
findings.location: §'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism (clears G6/T1/T3)' vs Remediation AC #2 ('The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace Remediation AC #2 with: 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out appends activated project criterion descriptors to the tier-split set inside `ProductionBatchRunner.run()` (after `route_criteria` returns the full effective set), without modifying `gates/plan-review.yaml` or the v3 schema; the static built-in criteria list in the YAML is untouched.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction: delete the `batch.extra_criteria` references from Remediation AC #2 and the ADR-contents bullet, and replace with the correct `ProductionBatchRunner`-based fan-out description (the body's stated approach).
findings.finding: Remediation AC #2 specifies 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list', but the plan body explicitly rules this out ('a new `batch` field is impossible without a v4 schema+shim') and adopts the `ProductionBatchRunner` approach instead. The ADR contents section additionally references 'the additive `batch.extra_criteria` fan-out'. An implementer cannot satisfy both simultaneously — the processing rule for how project criteria reach `Pass-1` is contradictorily specified at two plan locations.
findings.location: § Remediation AC bullet 2 ('The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list') vs. § Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism (clears G6/T1/T3) and § ADR contents
findings.suggested_fix: Strike 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list so an activated project criterion runs WITHOUT regenerating the static builtin fan-out.' from Remediation AC #2. Replace with: 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out appends `project.<name>`-prefixed descriptors inside `ProductionBatchRunner.run()` after the static criteria are resolved, deduped against built-ins; `gates/plan-review.yaml` and the v3 schema are untouched.' Update the ADR contents bullet to match.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting overlay-load + cache isolation (pure registry plumbing, no workflow touch) from the fan-out wiring + CI gate (workflow + runner change) into two sequenced leaf stories, or explicitly confirm that the combined scope fits one session and that all 12 ACs are reviewable together.
findings.finding: The plan carries 12 acceptance criteria (7 original + 5 remediation), touches 6+ named files across 4+ distinct architectural layers (overlay schema/JSON, cache/memo layer, registry/vocabulary layer, workflow-runner fan-out layer, CI validation gate, ADR), and introduces multiple new interfaces — this is above the single-session leaf threshold even accounting for the ANTI-FP on file lists.
findings.location: Scope section; Acceptance Criteria (both blocks); 'Files also touched' note in Scope clarification

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define who triggers `_invalidate_caches()` in the MCP server (e.g., on each RPC request, on a TTL schedule, or via explicit signal), and add a bounded-growth mechanism (e.g., `functools.lru_cache` with `maxsize`, or evict entries older than N versions) to prevent unbounded accumulation of (repo_root, hash) memo entries.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify who triggers `_invalidate_caches()` in the long-lived MCP server, nor does it bound stale-entry accumulation between calls: if many distinct (repo_root, overlay-hash) pairs are served over the server's lifetime, the memo dict grows without bound.
findings.location: Cache strategy section / `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` / AC: 'a RED cross-repo-leak test'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the plain dict with an `lru_cache`-backed or `functools.lru_cache(maxsize=128)` keyed function — this provides automatic LRU eviction. Alternatively, cap the module dict to the N most-recently-used entries using `collections.OrderedDict` with a fixed max size.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback for an activated project criterion with an `exec` type not supported in this story (DET, 2-STEP): either a located `RegistryError` at `load_criteria`/`effective_criteria` time with a message naming the unsupported type, or explicit documentation that only `exec: 1-TURN` and `exec: AGENT` are accepted in MVP.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for what happens when an activated project criterion has `exec: DET` or `exec: 2-STEP` at load-time. The plan guarantees the MVP exercises one `exec: 1-TURN` criterion, explicitly deferring `exec: DET` to story #2. But `load_criteria` / `effective_criteria` will encounter such a descriptor if a project author writes one. The plan says a missing prompt raises a `RegistryError`, but says nothing about an `exec`-type that the current implementation cannot handle — silent skip, located error, or passthrough to a code path that has no handler.
findings.location: § Scope ('Project exec: DET criteria … are story #2') and § Sanitization inverse & missing-prompt fallback (clears T8)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `effective_criteria` (or the overlay loader): 'an activated project criterion with `exec` not in {"1-TURN", "AGENT"} raises a located `RegistryError` naming the criterion id and the unsupported exec type.' Add a corresponding named test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the MVP E2E AC to require at least one test that exercises the `ProductionBatchRunner` fan-out path with a live (or contract-verified integration-level) LLM call, OR explicitly defer live coverage to a named child ticket with a documented rationale for why the offline substitute faithfully exercises the fan-out wiring.
findings.finding: The MVP end-to-end acceptance criterion ('activate one criterion → runs in plan-review Pass-1 → surfaces a finding') does not specify whether it must use a live LLM call or whether an offline/mocked LLM is acceptable. The new fan-out path runs through `ProductionBatchRunner`, which has a live external boundary (the LLM API). If the E2E test is satisfied by a mock/stub LLM that short-circuits the real batch runner, the actual production fan-out path (project descriptor appended to the tier-split set → real batch call → real LLM response → finding surfaced) is never exercised live, and the suite can go green while the live path is broken.
findings.location: AC: 'MVP slice demonstrated end-to-end (activate one criterion → runs → surfaces a finding)' and 'Remediation additions' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the MVP AC: 'The end-to-end test must invoke `ProductionBatchRunner` against a live or recorded-playback LLM endpoint (not a pure stub) and assert that the project criterion finding appears in the output; OR add an integration test tagged `@pytest.mark.live` that is run in CI on merge and is not gated by a mock flag.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency-safety mechanism for the module-level memo dicts (e.g., `threading.Lock` around check-then-set, or Python `dict.setdefault` with idempotent values) and for `_invalidate_caches()` clearing them atomically in a multi-threaded MCP server.
findings.finding: The module-level memo dicts (keyed by `(repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))`) are mutated by concurrent threads in the long-lived MCP server without any specified locking or atomic update strategy, creating a check-then-act TOCTOU window — especially during `_invalidate_caches()` which clears the dict while other threads may be reading or writing it.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `threading.Lock` per memo dict; acquire it for the full check-compute-insert and for the clear in `_invalidate_caches()`. Alternatively, use `dict.setdefault(key, _compute())` — but note this computes eagerly, so prefer the lock pattern. Document that double-computation is the accepted cost if you use a lock-free optimistic approach.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
